ElementDescription: The NDAR Global Unique Identifier (GUID) for research subject
Subject ID how it's defined in lab/project
Date on which the interview/genetic test/sampling/imaging/biospecimen was completed. MM/DD/YYYY
Age in months at the time of the interview/test/sampling/imaging.
Notes: Age is rounded to chronological month. If the research participant is 15-days-old at time of interview, the appropriate value would be 0 months. If the participant is 16-days-old, the value would be 1 month.

ElementDescription: Sex of subject at birth
Notes: M = Male; F = Female; O=Other; NR = Not reported

ElementDescription: Which day of the study is the participant on?
Time spent at home
Distance travelled
Radius of gyration
Maximum diameter: the longest distance between any two points
Maximum distance from home
Number of significant locations visited that day
Notes: Total number of locations in a day

ElementDescription: Average length of flight on that day
Standard deviation of the length of flights on that day
Average time flights took on that day
Standard deviation of the time flights took  on that day
Probability (fraction of time) a person is stationary (paused) during a day (as opposed to mobile/in flight)
Location entropy across a person's significant locations for the day
Number of minutes of missing data in a person's GPS mobility trace that day
Notes: Total number of minutes in a day

ElementDescription: Circadian routine measuring similarity in a person's locations over the course of a day to that of the other days in the data set.
Comparisons are stratified by grouping together weekends and grouping together weekdays into two separate groups.
Number of significant places visited at any point over the course of a day
Total time spent in flight over the course of a day (in hours)
Average of the length of all flights (straight line movement) that took place over the course of a day (in km)
Standard deviation of the length of all flights (straight line movement) that took place over the course of a day (in km)
Average of the duration of all flights (straight line movement) that took place over the course of a day (in hours)
Standard deviation of the duration of all flights (straight line movement) that took place over the course of a day (in hours)
Total time of all pauses that took place over the course of a day (in hours)
Average of the duration of all pauses that took place over the course of a day (in hour)
Standard deviation of the duration of all pauses that took place over the course of a day (in hours)
Entropy measure based on the proportion of time spent at significant locations over the course of a day

A continuous measurement of routine in the interval [0,1] that scores a day with 0 if there was a complete break from routine and 1 if the person followed the exact same routine as have in every other day of follow up
Physical circadian rhythm, but calculated with the procedure repeated separately for weekends and weekdays.
The total time when the GPS is on (in hours)
The total time when the GPS is on from 8AM to 8PM
The total time when the GPS is on from 8PM to 8AM
Time spent at home over the course of a day (in hours).
Total distance travelled over the course of a day (in km)
Largest distance between any two places that a person visited in a day (in km)
Largest distance between any places that a person visited in a day and their home (in km)
